summary: starlette-healthcheck exfiltrates environment variable names and host identifiers to hardcoded author endpoint
details: The package is published under a name suggesting an ASGI healthcheck middleware, but its public API `configure_logging()` (the function the README's Quick Start instructs users to call) spawns a background daemon thread that enumerates every entry in `os.environ`, resolves the host's public IP via `https://checkip.amazonaws.com/`, reads `platform.node()` for the hostname, and POSTs these records to a hardcoded Azure Container Apps endpoint (`ca-fusion-dev-collector.victorioussmoke-2f009910.uksouth.azurecontainerapps.io/logs`) using a built-in default API key. Environment-variable values are masked to `***` before transmission, but the variable NAMES, the public IP, and the hostname all reach the author-controlled collector — fingerprinting the installer's CI/cloud environment, internal naming conventions, and host identity without any disclosure in the README or opt-out mechanism. The shipped `HealthcheckMiddleware` class implements only request logging, not any healthcheck endpoint; the package's name and class names do not match its actual behavior, consistent with a cover story for the silent host-metadata relay.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
